item.title: CIBC: Contact Centre Representative (Credit Cards CARE, Remote)
item.description:
locations
Ontario VIRTUAL
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 10, 2024 (2 hours left to apply)
job requisition id
2419719
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.
To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com
What you'll be doing 
As a member of CIBC’s Contact Centre Credit Cards Department, you will be the first point of contact for clients calling in with questions or requests related to their banking needs, answering incoming calls from our credit cards clients as well as calls transferred from other departments.
As a Contact Centre Specialist II, Cards CARE you will assist clients with their credit cards operations and inquiries, identify their needs, and proactively suggest solutions to help them achieve their financial goals. You demonstrate solid written, verbal, and interpersonal skills to make a difference in clients’ lives, obtaining complete information on clients’ issues and summarizing and verifying the facts. The Contact Centre Specialist II, Cards CARE supports best practices, providing information and solutions to existing and potential clients regarding CIBC’s credit card products and services, including assisting clients with application and cancellation requests.
The role receives general instructions on routine work and requires limited direction, collaborating to find simple solutions that make it easy for clients to bank with us.
The start date for this role is Nov 18th, 2024.
Our Contact Centre is open Monday to Friday 7:00AM to Midnight, Saturday-Sunday 7:00AM to 10:00PM. This is a full-time and shift oriented role. We would like for you to be flexible between these hours.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. You can effectively perform all work activities remotely, and will only be required to be on-site on an occasional basis.
CIBC has embarked on an Agent@Home remote work program and all employees may be given the opportunity to work from home, if you can meet the Agent@Home program requirements.
Agent@Home Program Requirements
Ability to work independently as an Agent @ Home with a secure workspace, in a room or office with a door that closes it off from noises and distractions.
Wired Internet connection, capable of continuously supporting excellent call quality and high-speed response rate (Internet connection can be connected to modem through hard cable (not wireless) with a minimum download speed of 15 mbps and upload speed of 10 mbps).
Satellite internet providers are prohibited and cannot be used due to the impact on call quality and the stability of service.
How you'll succeed
Client engagement - Focus on each client experience and connect on a personal level to offer an exceptional client experience. Engage in client-focused interactions that are meaningful and deliver trusted advice. Provide support for clients by helping them to manage their accounts and products. Connect and refer clients to the right CIBC contacts to enable their financial success.
Problem solving - Listen, ask questions, and put yourself in the client’s shoes. Act like an owner by understanding the client`s needs and recommending solutions based on their needs. Remain solution-focused in order to identify opportunities to help our clients.
Efficiency – Understand that the client’s time is valuable and effectively complete their request. Strong knowledge of the CIBC systems and products to allow quick resolution of problems.
Product knowledge - Deeply understand CIBC’s suite of products and work with others to ensure clients are connected to the right people and opportunities. Proactively suggest banking options that will help clients achieve their financial goals. Understand and leverage other internal departments within CIBC to assist clients on additional needs.
Who You Are
You put our clients first. You engage with purpose to find the right solutions. You go the extra mile, because it’s the right thing to do.
You’re passionate about people. You find meaning in relationships, and surround yourself with a diverse network of partners. You build trust through respect and authenticity.
You love to learn. You’re passionate about growing your knowledge, and you know that there is no limit to what you can achieve.
You’re goal oriented. You’re motivated by accomplishing your goals and delivering your best to make a difference.
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values - trust, teamwork, and accountability.
What CIBC Offers
At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential. We aspire to give you a career, rather than just a paycheck.
We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive salary, incentive pay, banking benefits, a benefits program*, defined benefit pension plan*, an employee share purchase plan, a vacation offering, wellbeing support, and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
We cultivate a culture where you can express your ambition through initiatives like Purpose Day; a paid day off dedicated for you to use to invest in your growth and development.
*Subject to plan and program terms and conditions
What you need to know
CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com
You need to be legally eligible to work at the location(s) specified above and, where applicable, must have a valid work or study permit.
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
Job Location
Ontario VIRTUAL
Employment Type
Regular
Weekly Hours
37.5
Skills
Call Center, Client Service, Communication, Customer Experience (CX), Outbound Calls, Work Collaboratively

item.title: CIBC: Investor’s Edge, UI Designer Winter 2025 Co-Op (8-months)
item.description:
locations
Toronto, ON
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 21, 2024 (11 days left to apply)
job requisition id
2420133
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.
To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com
What you’ll be doing
As part of the CIBC Investor’s Edge UI team, you will be responsible for providing modern, visually stunning, and highly usable UI (user interface) experiences and creative solutions, and optimizing our online and mobile properties. In this role, you will work closely with other UI designers and will collaborate with UX, Editorial, Product and Technology. You will bring your passion for visual design and attention to detail, and use your expertise in typography, layout, colour theory and Figma to create pixel-perfect user interface elements with consistent look and feel. You will be part of Investor’s Edge’s Design System efforts collaborating closely with other UIs and developers to create single source of truth through global tokens and components.
Important information
You are enrolled in a post-secondary program in UI Design, Graphic Design, Interaction Design, or a related field at the time of the work term, with a graduation of December 2025 for later.
Please include your resume (no more than 2 pages), a cover letter (no more than 1 page), and your most recent unofficial transcript with your application. A portfolio showcasing UI design work samples, inclusive of unique and original designs is an asset.
Work Location: Toronto (hybrid with mandatory in-office on Mondays & Tuesdays)
Duration of Work Term: 8 months (January 2025 – August 2025)
Applications are being reviewed on a rolling basis, we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
How you’ll succeed
UI Design - Create high fidelity UI screens and components that align with the the UI kit, product’s experience goals, usability and accessibility standards, as well as brand guideline
Design System – Assist the core Design System team in creating and maintaining the global components with developers, and ensure consistency through design tokens and guidelines
Production Delivery - Ready technical specs in Figma, knowledge of Dev Mode an asset. You’ll be working collaboratively with developers/QA and key team members from various disciplines (e.g. editorial content, business analysis/product) within an agile/iterative design & development environment where timely delivery of design deliverables is critical.
Creativity - Use your creative thinking skill to to develop innovative and new solutions. Create protypes and presentation decks to share ideas and flows to peers and stakeholders.
Communication - Excellent verbal and written communication skills will be put to use to allow successful collaboration while working remotely in a team.
Time Management - Manage multiple concurrent objectives, project, groups and activities, while prioritizing and allocating time effectively.
Who you are
You act like an owner. You thrive when you're empowered to take initiative, go above and beyond, and deliver results.
You can demonstrate experience in or knowledge of Figma, and Microsoft Office tools. Working close knowledge (CSS, HTML) is an asset.
You're digitally savvy. You have strong visual design skills, including screen layout, composition, typography, color theory, illustration, and iconography.
You understand that success is in the details. You notice things that others don't. Your critical thinking skills help to inform your decision making.
You embrace and champion change. You'll continuously evolve your thinking and the way you work in order to deliver your best.
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values – trust, teamwork and accountability
What CIBC Offers
At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential.
We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive compensation, a banking benefit*, wellbeing support and additional offers such as employee and family assistance programs and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
*Subject to program terms and conditions
What you need to know
CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com
You need to be legally eligible to work at the location(s) specified above and, where applicable, must have a valid work or study permit
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
Expected End Date
2025-08-22
Job Location
Toronto-81 Bay, 14th Floor
Employment Type
Temporary (Fixed Term)
Weekly Hours
37.5
Skills
Cascading Style Sheets (CSS), Color Theory, Communication, English Composition, Figma, Hyper Text Markup Language (HTML), Microsoft Office, Teamwork, Typography, User Experience (UX), User Interface (UI) Design, User Interfaces (UI)

item.title: Manulife: Manager, Fund Events
item.description:
locations
Montreal, Quebec
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 2, 2024 (23 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24091767
The Manulife Fund Events team is seeking a manager to support the GWAM Canada business. In this role, the Manager will provide expertise and operational support to ensure successful implementation of the firm’s various initiatives impacting the Canadian Wealth Operations. This entails in-depth knowledge of fund operations and accounting in Canada & North America, a keen ability to form relationships with the impacted partners, strong analytical skills as well as the ability to think creatively.
The day-to-day will involve interacting with various business areas to understand the risk, financial and accounting impacts of business processes to best support collaborators. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to learn, develop and excel within a team that values client happiness, performance, teamwork, and innovation.
Position Responsibilities:
Coordinate and support the implementation of Canadian Operations projects and Initiatives up to and including post implementation support.
Ensure operational improvement projects are thoroughly detailed and enforce to standard processes
Identify operational or other business risks associated with initiative implementation and communicate them to leadership
Bring forth knowledge and expertise and ensure to communicate risks and impacts to the company operations and downstream
Ensure all fund system setups are aligned to the project requirements
Analyze, research and troubleshoot issues ranging from reconciliation breaks to client requests and changes
Liaise and communicate project requirements to custodian and valuation agent
Required Qualifications:
7+ years in the financial industry with proven experience
University degree preferably in accounting or finance CPA or CFA an asset
Experience and knowledge in fund accounting and back-office Operations
Good understanding of the financial services industry, investment funds, insurance funds and investment securities products
Preferred Qualifications:
Self-starter with excellent communication skills
Excellent interpersonal skills
Attention to detail
Ability to work on multiple projects in a fast-paced environment
Full knowledge and application of Microsoft Office suite
Bilingual (French & English) is required - The successful candidate will be required to communicate in English and French to support clients from various jurisdictions outside of Quebec.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Primary Location
Montreal, Quebec
Working Arrangement
Hybrid
Salary range is expected to be between
$81,450.00 CAD - $146,610.00 CAD
If you are applying for this role outside of the primary location, please contact recruitment@manulife.com for the salary range for your location. The actual salary will vary depending on local market conditions, geography and relevant job-related factors such as knowledge, skills, qualifications, experience, and education/training. Employees also have the opportunity to participate in incentive programs and earn incentive compensation tied to business and individual performance.
Manulife offers eligible employees a wide array of customizable benefits, including health, dental, mental health, vision, short- and long-term disability, life and AD&D insurance coverage, adoption/surrogacy and wellness benefits, and employee/family assistance plans. We also offer eligible employees various retirement savings plans (including pension and a global share ownership plan with employer matching contributions) and financial education and counseling resources. Our generous paid time off program in Canada includes holidays, vacation, personal, and sick days, and we offer the full range of statutory leaves of absence. If you are applying for this role in the U.S., please contact recruitment@manulife.com for more information about U.S.-specific paid time off provisions.

item.title: Manulife: Manager, Cybersecurity, Resilience, and Governance
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Boston, Massachusetts
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 27, 2024 (17 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24090908
The Opportunity
Work arrangement: Hybrid (3 days in office, 2 days from home)
Office locations: Toronto - Canada (primary) or Boston - USA
At John Hancock, we are hiring for Manager, Cybersecurity, Resilience, and Governance.
You'll work closely with our Cyber Defense Lead to drive robust security practices across the organization. You'll be deeply involved in application security, threat modeling, and you'll actively engage in hands-on assessments, integrating red teaming to identify vulnerabilities and strengthen our defenses while mentoring developers to foster a strong security culture. This is an exciting opportunity for a proactive leader who enjoys being hands-on with the keyboard and wants to make a significant impact on how we approach cybersecurity. If you’re ready to influence our security direction and champion best practices, we want you on our team
This role will serve as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) in application security, advocating for robust security practices, educating developers, and assisting with threat modeling. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in offensive security, proficiency in coding, tool development, and mentoring, demonstrating a well-rounded expertise in both technical and leadership aspects of cybersecurity, with the potential to develop new security solutions such as breach and attack simulations.
Position Responsibilities:
Strategic Cybersecurity Leadership: Provide expert guidance on designing and implementing cybersecurity measures for complex systems. Develop and promote security strategies including design principles and security architecture.
Application Security Expertise: Act as a SME in application security, advocating for best practices and helping to drive the security agenda within the organization. Educate developers on security principles, practices, and tools.
Threat Modeling and Assessment: Lead and support threat modeling activities to identify and assess potential security risks. Use threat modeling methodologies to guide the development and implementation of effective remediation strategies.
Security Portfolio Development: Explore and potentially build new security solutions, such as breach and attack simulations, to enhance the organization’s security posture and readiness.
Maintain, configure, and analyze security platforms and tools.
Serve as a subject matter expert on Cloud security, in both Azure/0365 and AWS.
Lead application vulnerability management efforts across the company's infrastructure and applications.
Collaboration and Communication: Work closely with cross-functional teams, including developers and security professionals, to integrate security practices into the development lifecycle. Articulate security risks, technical strategies, and outcomes effectively through strong verbal and written communication.
Presentation and Training: Develop and deliver presentations and training sessions to raise security awareness across the organization. Create and present engaging content tailored to various audiences, including technical teams and non-technical stakeholders, to promote understanding and adoption of security best practices.
Required Qualifications:
Must-Haves:
Proven track record in penetration testing/red teaming.
Strong knowledge of SAST, DAST, and application security practices.
Proficiency in a programming language (e.g., Python) and hands-on with security tools (e.g., Metasploit, Burp Suite, Cobalt Strike etc.).
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Security, or a related field.
At least 5 years of experience in cybersecurity, with expertise in one or more of the following areas: (1) application security, (2) threat modeling, (3) security architecture, (4) penetration testing, (5) ethical hacking, (6) vulnerability assessment, and (7) red teaming.
In-depth knowledge of application security testing techniques, including Static Application Security Testing (SAST), Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST), and Software Composition Analysis (SCA).
Excellent problem-solving skills, with the ability to manage complex security issues and drive creative solutions.
Demonstrated ability to manage stakeholders and communicate effectively at all levels of the organization.
Self-driven and capable of working independently with minimal supervision.
Flexibility to work onsite or remotely based on business needs.
Hands-on experience with Microsoft Azure.
Preferred Qualifications:
Advanced degrees or certifications (e.g., OSEP, OSWP, OSCP, CRTP, CRTO, CISSP, CISM) are a plus.
Strong background in offensive security and coding, with hands-on experience in penetration testing and security assessment tools.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.
#LI-JH
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Primary Location
Toronto, Ontario
Working Arrangement
Hybrid
Salary range is expected to be between
$92,190.00 CAD - $171,210.00 CAD
If you are applying for this role outside of the primary location, please contact recruitment@manulife.com for the salary range for your location. The actual salary will vary depending on local market conditions, geography and relevant job-related factors such as knowledge, skills, qualifications, experience, and education/training. Employees also have the opportunity to participate in incentive programs and earn incentive compensation tied to business and individual performance.
Manulife offers eligible employees a wide array of customizable benefits, including health, dental, mental health, vision, short- and long-term disability, life and AD&D insurance coverage, adoption/surrogacy and wellness benefits, and employee/family assistance plans. We also offer eligible employees various retirement savings plans (including pension and a global share ownership plan with employer matching contributions) and financial education and counseling resources. Our generous paid time off program in Canada includes holidays, vacation, personal, and sick days, and we offer the full range of statutory leaves of absence. If you are applying for this role in the U.S., please contact recruitment@manulife.com for more information about U.S.-specific paid time off provisions.

item.title: Manulife: Manager, Group Insurance Expenses
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 11, 2024 (1 day left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24080105
Reporting to the AVP, Head of Canada Expense Management, the Manager, Canada Segment Expenses is responsible for the implementation, maintenance and monitoring of monthly expenses, metric analysis and other expense accounting and reporting for Group Insurance business unit and functions assigned.
By using general expense expertise and business specific knowledge, the Expense Manager will provide analysis, accounting, and recommendations to their leader for reporting to senior management within the Canadian Segment as well as directly presenting these findings to the CFO, Group Insurance. This includes developing appropriate expense processes, monthly reporting as well as forecasting and participating to the annual budget process. The incumbent’s role is part of the broader Expense Shared Service Center (ESSC) and will work with peers from the different global expense functions.
Position Responsibilities:
Produce standard expense reports according to the schedule. Ensure quality and accuracy of data, reconciliations and adjustments as required. Provide valuable analysis and clear, concise commentary on expense results.
Investigate expense headwinds/tailwinds and provide analysis of trends to assist with business decisions.
Collaborate with business and expense function peers to deliver forecasts and budgets. This includes working closely with the functional work area managers within the assigned business unit to identify expense risk and opportunities and recommend potential actions for the BU CFO and representatives.
Build relationships across business and expense functions and establish a high level of credibility.
Maintain high standards in interactions with Business and Finance clients.
Provide strong, proactive leadership and coaching to indirect reports and extended team of financial professionals.
Use appropriate judgment in dealing with unexpected issues and requests, demonstrating an ability to prioritize work.
Identify and recommend best practices, process, and controls as well as supporting the development of suitable reporting.
Provide necessary project support to advance the objectives of the ESSC.
Required Qualifications:
Accounting designation or working towards (CMA, CA, CGA, CPA) with 5+ years of relevant experience in progressive accounting roles
Bachelor’s degree in accounting or finance or equivalent
Strong business acumen with ability to communicate complex financial concepts, analysis and results in a clear, concise, easy to understand manner including the ability to interact with senior leaders and representatives from BUs and functions
Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate and influence effectively
A comprehensive knowledge of reporting requirements, sound business practices and controls, and accounting principles which apply to financial companies and the area of expense management
Preferred Qualifications:
Self-starter with the ability to work independently, particularly in the area of using sound, independent judgment, problem-solving and analytical skills
Ability to independently manage multiple, conflicting priorities and deliver a quality result within tight deadlines
Strong accounting and expense systems knowledge (e.g. Essbase, CERT)
When you join our team:
We'll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We'll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of the global team, we'll support you in the shaping the future you want to see.
#LI-Hybrid

item.title: Manulife: IT Controls Governance Specialist
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 17, 2024 (7 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24090963
The opportunity
You will enable your business and IT partners to recognize and manage information risk in an ambitious and dynamic business environment. You will provide governance and support of IT Audits with a focus on SOX. This is an outstanding opportunity to lead in a field that is dynamic and critical to our business operations. Your expertise will help us deliver top-notch IT controls governance and successfully implement comprehensive risk management strategies.
Responsibilities
Collaborate with IT teams to ensure alignment to and detailed recording of IT General Control processes.
Assist IT teams in the maintenance of control process flow documentation.
Coordinate evidence collection and perform quality control on evidence provided to audit.
Manage meetings with control owners, internal audit, and external audit.
Advance any delays to people leaders when required.
Report on progress related to audit, issues, and corrective action plans.
Facilitate training and governance to assist IT teams in successfully communicating with internal and external auditors.
Collaborate with team members to develop solutions for potential issues.
Evaluate and address the underlying causes of issues with customers, partners, and team members to mitigate risk and establish effective and verifiable action plans.
Help manage the divisional IT SOX plan to account for deviations from the company IT SOX framework and planned material changes to SOX applications.
Support technology risk assessments working alongside IT, cybersecurity, and others.
Collaborate with business and IT to prioritize ITGC's.
Collaborate with other divisional/segment teams for a successful global outcome.
Other duties as assigned.
How will you build impact?
You will show our customers, collaborators, and regulators that important internal and third-party IT services are delivered with effective information risk controls. You will report to a director and regularly interact with colleagues in systems support and governance roles, their managers, and senior management. Your commitment to following controls and governance standards will guarantee seamless service delivery and contribute to our reputation for excellence.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage, and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your skills to enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.
What we are looking for
Five years or more of progressive experience in managing information risks across various fields: IT auditing, IT risk and control assessments, technology/vendor risk assessment, network security, access control, infrastructure/platform security, data/application security, Change management, IT Operations.
Professional certification or designation in information security (e.g., CISM, CISSP) or IT auditing (e.g., CISA) or a related field preferred.
University Degree or equivalent experience preferred.
Strong personal computing skills (MS Office, MS SharePoint) and familiarity with GRC (i.e Archer) and change management tools (i.e ServiceNow, Git)
Superb communication skills (oral and written) including presentation skills and demonstrated ability to present at all organizational levels.
Knowledge of audit methodologies, project management and system development methodologies, control frameworks and risk management practices.
Flexibility in working hours due to the company’s global footprint.
Working knowledge of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002.
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.

Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer

We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. item.title: Manulife: Credit Risk Senior Analyst
item.description:
locations
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 30, 2024 (20 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100052
Reporting to the Director, Account Management, Specialized Lending, the Credit Risk Senior Analyst is a key part of a team of Account Management professionals whose primary role is to complete accurate & concise annual reviews of commercial & personal loans with assurance that all of Manulife Bank’s credit risk policies, procedures and regulatory guidelines are being adhered to.
This job description does not represent a comprehensive listing of all job duties that are required of the employee performing this role.
Responsibilities
Responsible for the annual review of a portfolio of Specialized Lending loans through the gathering and evaluation of insurance & Bank documentation, ensuring compliance to company policies, standards & procedures and adherence to all financial industry regulations.
Review of loans using an established review methodology, assist in identifying actual and potential loan issues at the earliest possible time; loans that are not compliant are highlighted and actioned in accordance with file escalation processes.
Request, review, & evaluate documentation provided by the advisor and/or client required to complete annual reviews; ensuring loan reviews are conducted in a timely, accurate and professional manner.
Investigate, evaluate, and analyze documentation provided to ensure all legal, Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know-Your-Client (KYC) legislation requirements have been satisfied and refer suspected fraud to internal business partners.
Track, document, and update all assigned reviews, completed reviews, & in process reviews, with monthly completion targets for annual reviews.
Recommend changes to policy, standards, operational processes, and procedures as needed based on deficiencies or gaps noted through the loan review process.
Other activities include completion of loan withdrawal requests, updates/requests to Credit Risk Management, and completion of other customer enquiries in a timely manner.
Build and maintain professional relationships and work collaboratively with all lines of business within the Bank, including Account Management, Sales, Account Support, and Credit Risk Management.
How will you create impact?
The Credit Risk Senior Analyst will play a pivotal role in maintaining the Specialized Lending loan portfolio through primarily the completion of annual loan reviews, as well as assisting the team with other portfolio activities and requests.
You will have a real impact on ensuring the Bank is maintaining its Specialized Lending portfolio in a sound fashion, working in a dynamic, fast-paced environment with partners across several different areas of the Bank.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
What we are looking for
3- 5 years (minimum) of experience in underwriting/loan review/credit review of Personal and/or Commercial loan products
Firm grasp of lending credit principles and ability to comprehend insurance policy documents; experience with financial statement analysis is an asset.
Strong communication skills, including the ability to convey complex ideas clearly and concisely.
Demonstrated ability to prioritize and manage multiple assignments in a fast-paced environment to meet deadlines with efficiency and accuracy
Exceptional interpersonal skills, with the confidence, credibility, and ability to work effectively within the organization
High level of professionalism, self-motivation and sense of urgency with the ability to work successfully with minimal supervision.
Demonstrated ability to use MS Office (MS Word and Excel and internet-based communication mediums) – knowledge of Manulife Bank systems is an asset.
Exemplary proficiency (spoken and written) in English (with French bilingualism being an asset for this role).
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.
Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.

We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.

item.title: Manulife: Manager, Tax
item.description:
locations
Boston, Massachusetts
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 25, 2024 (15 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24051225
This Manager, Tax position will be responsible for providing tax support to the Private Equity Infrastructure Unit. This will include working closely with the Infrastructure tax team as well as the Business Unit on all tax related matters for existing and future Funds such as managing and reviewing all Federal and State Income tax filings, assisting in the preparation and reviewing tax provisions and related disclosures, performing tax research on technical matters, assisting tax legal where applicable, calculate and report tax withholding, providing tax related information to investors including estimates, work with internal and external auditors, manage external service providers, manage information with respect portfolio companies and calculate and report carry. This role will also be involved with new strategic initiatives and participate in resolving complex technical issues for the assigned area.
Position Responsibilities:
Federal and State Tax Returns
Manage and review federal and state income tax returns, supporting documentation, and disclosures. Including federal and state extensions and payments.
Assists with technical review of transactions.
Assists with tax law change analysis.
International Tax Compliance:
Manage and review information related to VAT tax, and other foreign filings when applicable.
Tax Withholding and reporting
Manage the indirect reporting process which includes:
Collecting and working with outside vendor to vet withholding tax documents
Calculate withholding and provide information to infrastructure operations team.
Provide answers to Investor questions promptly.
Work with outside vendor to produce and report year end filing requirements (1042S)
Provisions: Assist in the Review of Fund tax provisions including monitoring state tax rates.
State related accruals, and state inquiries and audits where applicable
Administration: provide tax fee budgets to operations team as well as review and manage the payment of external fees.
Goal setting & delivering results:
Participates in strategic and tactical goal setting for the group and is accountable for delivering results.
Responsible for performance-management related activities, such as goal setting, monitoring goal completion, on-going coaching, performance appraisals, etc.
Participates in ongoing professional education.
Required Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in accounting or finance required.
Master’s degree in Tax or commensurate education / experience desired
C.P.A. (Certified Public Accountant) or similar designation
5+ years of tax experience with at least 5+ years dealing with corporate and partnership tax or relevant issues. Supervisory experience preferred.
Advanced proficiency of financial accounting, reporting, and compliance, computation of Federal taxable income and Federal tax, industry specific tax rules, taxation of transactions, state, local taxation concepts and procedures, understanding of technology that supports all tax functions, partnership concepts and tax matters.
Knowledge in private equity structures is required.
Advanced organizational, communication and collaboration skills
Proficiency with managing various workstreams and deadlines simultaneously.
Effectively uses experience and business knowledge to solve problems and contribute to organizational goals.
Advanced research skills, solves problems independently.
Preferred Qualifications:
Empowered to prepare, analyze, and review assigned tax filings, provisions and various other tax calculations and is accountable for delivering accurate and timely results.
Review and sign-off on check and e-treasury requests
Participates in strategic and tactical goal setting for specific projects and is accountable for delivering results.
Provide updates to Tax and Business Leadership
Empowered to make decisions on and responsible for tax operational requirements.
When you join our team:
We'll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We'll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of the global team, we'll support you in the shaping the future you want to see.

item.title: Manulife: VP, Employee Experience, Technology
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 15, 2024 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100499
The Vice President of Employee Experience Technology will provide comprehensive technical leadership across a range of infrastructure, applications, and business solutions. This role supports Human Capital Management (HCM), Payroll Processing, Communications, and Employee Services for Manulife’s Global Human Resources & Communications Management teams. Positioned within the Technology team, the VP will collaborate closely with HR & Communications as primary stakeholders, as well as with Technology and infrastructure teams, to provide employees with the best capabilities, including the adoption of self-serve GenAI tools.
Position Responsibilities:
Employee Experience Technology:
Strategic Leadership: Identify and evaluate emerging HR & Communication technology trends, both internally and externally, to formulate value propositions and actionable strategies for aligning application portfolios.
Productivity Enhancement: Develop and implement an operations productivity roadmap focusing on Citizen Development and maximizing the adoption of GenAI tools to ensure employees have the right tools to excel.
Systems Strategy: Collaborate with HR and Communications leadership to plan and develop the HR and Communications systems and capabilities roadmap. This includes aligning the Workday platform with Manulife’s strategy and ensuring seamless integration across multiple systems, vendors, and platforms.
Optimization Initiatives: Lead efforts to optimize Manulife’s HR technologies, systems, and platforms (e.g., Workday) to eliminate manual work, drive efficiency, ensure compliance, and enhance the employee experience.
Data Management: Establish a strong data management foundation governing data architecture, quality, and integration, while building long-term capabilities such as a sustainable HR data repository to facilitate quick analysis of talent data.
Unified HR Services: Provide architectural and technology leadership to unify Manulife’s HR services platform, integrating HR portal, communications, mobile delivery, case management, knowledge, and chat capabilities.
Global Processes: Design, implement, and communicate global processes to drive value and adoption across the business.
Team Development: Develop the HR & Communications Technology team to ensure deep bench strength and expertise in key areas.
People Management:
Team Leadership: Lead a team of full-time and contract staff, overseeing goal planning, achievement assessments, and employee development plans.
Resource Planning: Collaborate with the broader Group Functions Technology & Global Data management teams to determine resource requirements and planning.
Skill Development: Encourage cross-training and job sharing, creating an annual plan for skill development and advanced technical capabilities.
Stakeholder Engagement: Lead workshops, architecture & engineering meetups, user groups, and meet with key stakeholders globally to build capabilities for Group Functions and across Manulife.
Governance and Controls:
Process Alignment: Ensure all technology management processes align with standard operating procedures and Group Functions architecture and enterprise technology standards and policies.
Technology Guidance: Provide guidance on technology principles, capabilities, practices, security, and access controls.
Innovation and Continuous Improvement:
Thought Leadership: Offer thought leadership and guidance on advancing new technologies and capabilities that are value driven.
Industry Advancements: Stay current with industry advancements in Human Resources & Communications and collaborate with Manulife’s innovation teams and other technology teams.
Stakeholder Communication: Maintain regular communication with HRLT stakeholders, providing metrics, KPIs, and service levels to address challenges, new business, issues, prioritization, and budget updates.
Required Qualifications:
Experience: 10-15 years of professional experience in Human Resources Technology, with a degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field.
Project Management: Advanced skills in project management methodologies (DevOps, Extreme Programming, Agile, Waterfall).
Strategic Execution: Proven ability to formulate and execute a holistic data and technology strategy for Human Resources.
HR Systems Expertise: Solid understanding of HR systems (e.g., Workday, ADP, Salesforce), business, data, and technical architecture disciplines.
Technical Translation: Ability to translate non-technical user requests into complex technical specifications.
Enterprise Architecture: Experience leading complex architecture initiatives and delivering enterprise-scale solution architectures.
Preferred Qualifications:
Visionary Leadership: Strategic thinker with a proactive approach to leading the HR tools and technology roadmap.
Stakeholder Management: Outstanding skills in partnering with HR stakeholders to deliver a joint vision.
Organizational Skills: Excellent organizational and time management skills, with the ability to multitask and work with minimal supervision.
Communication: Exceptional oral, written, and interpersonal communication skills, adept at simplifying complex technical concepts.
Relationship Building: Strong ability to establish working relationships across business, operations, and technology partners.
Adaptability: Quick to ramp up in new business and technology areas.
Proactive Attitude: Highly motivated, self-directed, and proactive.
Travel: Willingness to travel up to 40% of the time (primarily between Toronto, Waterloo, Boston, Hong Kong/Manila).
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.
About Manulife and John Hancock

item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Montreal, Quebec
time type
Full time
posted on

time left to apply
End Date: November 8, 2024 (29 days left to apply)

JR24100228
We are passionate about building and facilitating a long-term content strategy for owned and third-party channels that supports all business lines within Canada Segment, positions Manulife Canada as a partner in health, and tells the wider “Why Manulife” story.
Key Accountabilities:
· Work with the Director to develop key editorial narratives for each business line and plan out a long-term, multi-platform, always-on content strategy for Canada Segment
· Work cross functionally across business lines to build a coordinated content calendar that priorities across Canada Segment and tells our well-rounded brand story
· Ideate, assign and edit content crafted to enforce our brand narrative and support our marketing goals, ensuring consistent editorial tone/voice
· Develop and monitor success metrics for content
· Work closely with SEO to determine a segment-specific search strategy, including opportunities to guide content development and optimization of existing content.
· Obsess about audiences, what they want and need from content, and the best ways to reach them
· Manage a small team of content creators for Canada Segment striving to produce, outstanding content to drive brand awareness, foster customer engagement, and generate business leads
· Work with marketing heads and digital team to identify best ways to amplify/drive to content
· Work with social media editor to ensure editorial voice is reflected in our social media content
Capabilities/Experience:
• Solid understanding of the Canadian Financial and Insurance space
• Experience developing content strategies in support of business priorities, including vehicles for thought leadership
• Ability to write and edit short, medium and long-form multi-media content under tight deadlines
• Experience analyzing digital metrics and using data to guide planning
• Develops and maintains reporting dashboards using Adobe Analytics
• Understanding of writing for SEO and SEO strategy
• Excellent consulting, presentation and communication skills
• Self-starter who thrives in a fast-paced environment and can handle multiple contending priorities
• Highly collaborative, works well with cross-functional partners
• Solid understanding of how to navigate a large organization and manage relationships with external partners
Required Qualifications:
Proven experience in editorial, brand content or agency environment, including crafting content and building strategy for B2B and B2C audiences.
• Demonstrated ability to manage and develop a team of writers and content creators
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

item.title: Manulife: Senior Solution Architect
item.description:
locations
CAN, Ontario, Toronto, 200 Bloor Street East
CAN, Ontario, Waterloo, 500 King Street North
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 25, 2024 (15 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24071650
Our Canadian Enterprise Architecture team is looking to expand our practice!
We are looking for flexible, resilient, energetic IT professionals with an engineering mentality, who understands how to deliver what the customer wants and needs, through great collaboration with our business and IT partners.
We encourage you to apply if you are passionate and experienced with the different pillars we are building on the team:
Enterprise Architecture and design
Integration
Cloud design and Implementation
Cybersecurity
How will you contribute?
Partner with dedicated IT professionals to craft and implement cross-platform solutions to support our customers as they engage with the business processes.
Are you motivated to tackling sophisticated cases while being at the forefront of the technology journey; helping with the transformation of our Canadian business? Join our team!
Strong background in Optical Character Recognition (OCR) technologies, including understanding various OCR techniques, tools, and frameworks.
In-depth knowledge of Azure Vision AI services, including Computer Vision, OCR, and other related services within the Azure ecosystem.
Proficiency in machine learning and deep learning concepts, especially as they relate to image processing and recognition tasks.
Experience in designing and architecting AI solutions, understanding system scalability, reliability, and performance considerations.
Have a stake in the broader technical ecosystem and the solutions that you own by acting as a member of the teams you work with, using standards and patterns as an accelerator.
Be data driven in both decision making and in governance practices.
Hold on demand reviews in public for greater transparency, ensuring that governance is collaborative and not punitive.
Learn how to measure critical metrics, understand their value, and then use the results to continuously improve both the ecosystem and your solutions.
Develop useful architecture models that can be used by both system architects and engineers, and demoing these models.
Partner with product collaborators by influencing priorities while being responsible for the technical solutions.
Be proactive, working with collaborators to create architecture concepts prior to initiating an effort, demoing changes to these concepts.
Develop and maintain future models to align business and technology strategy, and any product purchases or vendor assessments.
Keep models updated frequently and available for others to access.
Attributes:
7+ years proven experience as a Solution Architect
Demonstrated ability engineering large scale and sophisticated systems from design to production which involve AI.
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
Ability to translate sophisticated business problems and important metrics into a solution architecture which can be demoed and implemented by multiple agile teams.
Have an eye for the future - we need leaders with vision who flourish with learning about what's on the horizon and seeing how it can be applied to the customer challenges we need to address.
You know and care about writing safe, sound, and secure code and have fixed issues yourself.
Experience with and curiosity about going deep on topics such as continuous delivery, reliability, security, cloud, and data science.
What can we offer:
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment:
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all partners and has a positive social and environmental impact.

item.title: Manulife: Senior Platform Engineer
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 27, 2024 (17 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24070300
The Opportunity
Manulife is seeking a self-motivated Platform Engineer for Data and Analytics Platforms, who is obsessed with delivering value, is forward-thinking, and optimizing infrastructure! You will be responsible to build, handle and support DnA platforms.

Responsibilities
Develop and build the cloud automation workflow and support the cloud blueprint.
Support hands-on cloud platform provisioning and configuration management coding development, deployment
Deliver business cloud solution by implementing the cloud resources (provisioning, hardening, etc.)
Continuously improve the monitoring and logging engineering for cloud area and enable the end-to-end monitoring and dashboard reporting
Standardize the runbook by automating the standard BAU tasks
Create, maintain the operational/user documentation for engineering product/solution
Build coding for platform automation and scripting for the runbook to 7x24 support and monitoring teams.
Conduct new cloud platform testing, runbook, and packing as cloud service offering to business users.
Plan and implement vital controls/activities for infrastructure and platform changes.
Assist to plan, implement, and review the capacity planning (any limits) for resources and report/recommend to management if required.
May involve for implementing some of the DR/DR drill for different markets according to local compliance and BCP requirement if required from RUN team
Support the architecture team to support their design and configure the data and analytics landscapes

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
Minimum of 7+ years of experience in leading a large implementation of related technologies
Hands-on expertise on Azure Cloud IaaS and PaaS management such as provisioning, scripting etc. in both day 1 and day 2 operation.
Expertise on all DevOps tools set and IaC programming such as Terraform, Python, PowerShell for infrastructure automation management.
Proven experience on common CICD pipeline, repositories management such as Github actions, Jenkins, etc.
Experience in implementing and maintaining large-scale data and analytics systems.
Knowledge on infrastructure services provisioning, security concepts, standard methodologies on the IaaS and PaaS models to support data and analytic solutions built.
Experience with compliance and audit activities for enforcement of policies, standards, and procedures
Familiarity with scripting and programming (Python, batch, PowerShell, shell script etc.,)
Understanding of and experience with ITIL processes
A solid understanding of risk, security, and compliance requirements and standard methodologies
Good understanding of networking infrastructure and network fixing (TCP/IP, DNS, routing, switching, firewalls, LAN/WAN, DHCP, Load balancing, Replication, SSH)
Able to support enterprise infrastructure and assist in the day-to-day operation of the team
Strong and reliable teammate with the ability to handle change and work independently
Passion on adopting and testing the cloud and new IT technology
Good collaborative attitude, willingness to work and share knowledge/information with team members
Solid understanding of project scheduling and task driven project work
Strong communication and technical skills, prior experience with mentoring teams and working in a process driven environment
Effective troubleshooting/ problem solving skills
Able to deliver the job on time by prioritizing the tasks/problems.
Excellent communication, and collaboration skills
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment to you
Values-first culture: We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity: We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation: We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion: We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship: We build a business that benefits all partners and has a positive social and environmental impact.

item.title: Manulife: Head of Sales Enablement
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 17, 2024 (7 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24091089
Reporting to the Head of Retail Distribution, we are looking for a Head of Sales Enablement to join our team and lead the charge in developing and driving innovative sales enablement strategies. In this essential position, you will empower our sales team to achieve their objectives and performance targets, using sales performance data to provide actionable insights and strategic recommendations. You will play a crucial role in supporting the implementation and continuous monitoring of our distribution strategy, ensuring it aligns with our overarching business goals. As the primary representative for Distribution on various committees and initiatives, your ability to communicate and consider Distribution's needs and perspectives will be key.
Position Responsibilities:
Develop and implement sales enablement strategies to empower the sales team in reaching their objectives and performance targets and analyze sales performance data to provide actionable insights and strategic recommendations for enhancing sales processes and strategies.
Support the implementation and continuous monitoring of the distribution strategy, offering recommendations for adjustments as necessary.
Act as the primary representative for Distribution in committees and initiatives, ensuring their needs and perspectives are effectively communicated and considered.
Support the development and updates of new products by ensuring Distribution’s needs and perspectives are integrated and collaborate with other Business Units, including GWAM, to find opportunities for bringing to bear existing partnerships to drive distribution growth across the Enterprise.
Lead and oversee the Insurance Field Readiness program, collaborating with internal and external partners to ensure effective readiness for distribution partners, advisors, and the sales team and serve as the key liaison for Distribution in relation to other market segment strategies, focusing on Mass Market and HNW segments.
Lead the development, implementation, and ongoing oversight of a comprehensive firm and advisor segmentation strategy for GIP, Insurance, and Vitality.
Required Qualifications:
10 to 15 years of experience in the financial services industry with deep knowledge of Individual Insurance and Investment products and sales strategies.
CFP, CLU, CHFC, CHS designations are an asset.
Excellent understanding of Manulife insurance products, concepts, administration, systems, and value proposition.
In-depth understanding of the Canadian insurance competitive environment.
Strong understanding of distribution and the advisor experience.
Proven ability to negotiate and influence effectively throughout the organization.
Proficiency in change leadership and implementation skills.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

item.title: Manulife: Senior Reporting Steward/ Project Analyst
item.description:
locations
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 23, 2024 (13 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24030517
The Canadian segment Reporting Steward function contributes to the success of the end-to-end operation of the IFRS 17/9 Financial Reporting process in Canadian segment. The Reporting Steward team works closely with a wide variety of people at the Group and segment levels including actuarial valuation, accounting, various IT teams and the business to ensure that the quarter-end process runs smoothly. The incumbent is a valued member who will act as the Reporting Steward for several (but not all) lines of business in Canadian segment. They will develop a deep understanding of the end-to-end reporting process and be able to trouble shoot production issues as well as coordinate various audit requirements and disclosure reports. In addition to quarter-end responsibilities, the incumbent will also play a crucial role in ensuring that changes to the financial reporting process are implemented appropriately within the respective lines of business that they support. This includes working with a cross functional team to gather, document, facilitate prioritization, and monitor the implementation of changes to the reporting process.
Position Responsibilities:
Support the financial reporting change management process by:
maintaining a long-term marco view of financial reporting projects within their purview and proactively engage with stakeholders in a timely manner to ensure effective implementation
facilitate the prioritization of the quarterly implementation list of projects within their purview
monitor the development and testing of items as they move towards final implementation
Develop a deep understanding of the end-to-end reporting process and be able to trouble-shoot production issues within their purview
Coordinate various audit requirements and disclosure reports during quarter end within their purview
Required Qualifications:
Successful experience managing projects in a dynamic, fast-paced environment
5+ years of experience in the financial service industry with experience in financial reporting
Excellent organization and time management skills and the ability to manage competing priorities while demonstrating sound judgment
Superior written, verbal communication skills and interpersonal skills; able to influence and negotiate with management at various levels of the organization. Build strong relationship with key stakeholders
Ability to analyze complex situations quickly and appreciate the balance between business needs, system constraints and risk implications
Preferred Qualifications:
Project Management certification. Accounting and/or actuarial designations is an asset
Strong desire to learn and engage with finance reporting subject matter experts to expand knowledge and effectively advocate for the end-to-end Financial Reporting teams
When you join our team:
We'll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We'll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.

item.title: Manulife: Client Service Specialist - AdminAdvantage
item.description:
locations
Waterloo, Ontario
Kitchener, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 28, 2024 (18 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24010461
The AdminAdvantage Administration team within the Manulife Group Benefits organization links people, technology, and expert services to simplify benefits administration and provide a consistent, powerful member and client experience. Our vision is to be recognized as the partner of choice within the industry for benefits solutions and benefits administration services.
Our clients trust the administration of their group benefits to us, and you will be part of a team that delivers outstanding results to those clients and our members.
If you enjoy working in a fast-paced, changing environment where every day brings something new and innovative ideas are encouraged, you may be the candidate we are looking for !
Responsibilities
Lead end-to-end client experience within the assigned portfolio, engaging directly with clients to anticipate and respond to their needs.
Collaborate with internal team members and stakeholders to resolve client issues and provide counsel.
Take ownership of implementing Benefits Administration on our online platform and participate in implementation and re-enrollment project meetings.
Act as a project manager and attend training to stay current with system and product enhancements.
Assist with procedure development and act as the point of contact for escalated requests from internal and external customers.
Identify client needs and perceptions, developing action plans to ensure a positive client and member experience.
How will you create impact?
Plan Sponsor Administration is seeking a Client Service Specialist to join our AdminAdvantage team. The successful candidate will be a highly motivated, influential, and passionate professional who is committed to building proven relationships while delivering on initiatives in a fast-paced and changing environment.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
What we are looking for
Handling a dedicated portfolio of clients in a fast-paced environment, applying technical industry knowledge and adapting to technology.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to multi-task and work in a high-volume, deadline-oriented environment.
Extensive knowledge of Group Benefits and AdminAdvantage is an asset.
Bilingual (French & English) is a strong asset. If bilingual, the successful candidate will ideally be required to communicate in both English and French to support clients from diverse jurisdictions outside of Quebec.
Proactively taking action to address problems and driving continuous improvement through digitization, innovation, and automation.
Prioritizing customer satisfaction, clear communication, resilience, and collaboration with various stakeholders.
Demonstrating strong judgement in balancing business risk with delivering results and possessing strong Office 365 skills.
What can we offer you?
The working schedule is Monday to Friday from 8:30 am to 4:30 pm, with flexibility to accommodate client needs.
A competitive salary and benefits package.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.

item.title: Manulife: Marketing Manager
item.description:
locations
Boston, Massachusetts
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 25, 2024 (15 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24091495
We're looking for a new Marketing Manager for our Global Wealth & Asset Management (GWAM) business to coordinate, develop and implement integrated marketing campaigns across multiple asset classes, distribution channels, and media to support sales efforts.
Individual Responsibilities:
Coordinate and implement marketing materials/campaigns to support products and programs on time and within budget.
Research, gather, and disseminate information for projects
Write and present integrated marketing plans that support sales efforts and generate repeatable results.
Recommend and develop appropriate communication vehicles for achieving marketing objectives
Work with other departments to source information and data for projects across media (digital, print, email, etc.)
Develop and maintain relationships across all departments, including sales, product, legal, compliance, and where applicable, external advisors and distributors.
Collaborate with product management, internal and external sales management to ensure alignment of efforts.
Monitor and report on effectiveness of ongoing/existing sales and marketing programs relative to competitor activities and current market conditions.
Manage the development of materials for new and existing products and programs including advertising, sales brochures, sales ideas, e-mails, web, direct mail and training pieces ensuring materials support business and marketing strategies.
Manage operational processes to facilitate marketing campaigns such as compliance review, material creation and quality improvement.
Creatively and effectively manage all media to drive marketing campaigns
Track ROI, evaluate campaign success using analytics.
Create, track and manage budgets for areas of oversight
Develop and deliver effective presentations to key stakeholders.
Knowledge, Skills and Experience requirements:
Bachelor's Degree is required.
3-8 years of proven experience in a marketing manager or account management role.
Experience in retirement, mutual fund industry, and/or investment industry is highly desired.
Ad or digital agency experience is a plus.
Series 6 or 7 is beneficial but not required
Knowledge of industry, markets, investments and investment concepts
Excellent interpersonal and prioritization skills, including the ability to meet deadlines
Capacity to work in a fast-paced, often changing environment.
Excellent communication and presentation skills
Creative and innovative problem solver with a solution-oriented approach.
Ability to build and maintain positive relationships at all levels of the organization
Demonstrated experience handling internal and external client relationships.
Ability to work independently and collaborate within a team environment.
Ability to persuade, handle time, prioritize effectively and be results oriented.
Comfortable using and learning new technologies.
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits package
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills
A focus on growing your career path with us
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance
Professional development and leadership opportunities

Values-first culture: We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
