item.title: Manulife: Brand Marketing Specialist
item.description:
locations
Makati City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 20, 2024 (9 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100175
The opportunity
The Brand Marketing Specialist will see an opportunity to change the way content marketing is done in the insurance industry and has the drive and talent to make it happen. As a pivotal member of the team, you will collaborate closely with the Brand Lead to spearhead the development and execution of comprehensive 360 marketing strategies. Your expertise will be instrumental in engaging diverse audiences across multiple platforms, driving creative production, ensuring brand governance, and managing marketing operations. This is an exciting opportunity to make a significant impact on the Manulife brand and contribute to our corporate social responsibility initiatives.
Individual Accountabilities:
Participate in brainstorms and help develop ideas for creative marketing campaigns
Assist in marketing activities by demonstrating expertise in various areas (content development and optimization, advertising, events planning, etc.)
Liaise with external partners to execute promotional events and campaigns
Collaborate with marketing teams to coordinate brand awareness and marketing efforts
Help plan and execute initiatives to reach the target audience through appropriate channels (social media, display ads, etc.)
Assist in analyzing marketing data (reach, traffic, etc.) to help shape future marketing strategies
Undertake individual tasks of a marketing plan as assigned
Help grow and maintain a list of vendor contacts
Key Shared Accountabilities:
Performance of brand campaigns in partnership with local teams and cross- functional regional teams.
Management and implementation of the Global Brand Strategy

How will you create impact?

The role directly impacts our Brand Power and Brand Awareness scores by driving the right intermediary KPIs. The role reports to the Head of Brand, Content, & Digital who reports to the Philippines CMO.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
Strong understanding of digital marketing and proven experience working with such teams to connect content to a company’s digital marketing activity.
Proven experience in developing and executing 360 marketing strategies, with a strong emphasis on brand building and creative direction.
Exceptional analytical skills with the ability to measure, report, and optimize campaign performance against KPIs and ROI.
Demonstrated ability to ideate and produce compelling creative content across various formats (e.g., digital videos, articles, ATL/BTL collaterals).
Experience in managing brand compliance and ensuring consistency in all marketing materials and campaigns.
Strong organizational and multitasking abilities to manage multiple projects and deadlines effectively.
Proven experience in budget management, financial reporting, and vendor coordination.
Proactive, innovative, and results-driven mindset with a passion for continuous improvement and marketing innovation.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate effectively with internal teams, external partners, and creative agencies.
Experience in sourcing and managing Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) and brand ambassadors.
Minimum 5 years’ experience in brand management roles within an agency and/or a brand. Experience in Financial services (insurance/bank/investments) is a plus provided you can still think outside of the category norm.
Fluent in both written and spoken English.

What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.

We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact

item.title: Manulife: Senior Procurement Specialist
item.description:
locations
Quezon City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: December 31, 2024 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24091675
Are you looking for a supportive and collaborative workplace with great benefits and clear career development? You’ve come to the right place.
Why choose Manulife?
Competitive Salary packages and performance bonuses
Day 1 HMO + FREE coverage for your dependents (inclusive of same-sex partners)
Retirement savings benefit
Rewarding culture that values wellness and well-being
Performance Bonus
Global network of industry experts
Extensive training resources
Job Description:
We’re looking for a Senior Procurement Specialist to join Global Investment Product Team within Procurement Operations group at MBPS. In this role, you are oversight of the end-to-end execution of the sourcing & contracting process for various spend categories for NA sourcing initiatives driving savings targets and other organizational goals
Have the skills and knowledge for the job? Learn more about the opening below!
Responsibilities
Conduct detailed analysis and develop relevant sourcing and negotiation strategy for assigned projects and Generate and leverage market and supplier intelligence and data for development of sourcing and negotiation strategies
Submit RFx (RFI, RFQ, RFP or e-Auction) requests to suppliers and make recommendations to the business partners on which providers will meet the commercial print or digital communication needs
Participate during the contract negotiation process, with consultative support, as required, from legal and risk
Perform other related strategic sourcing activities and participate in special projects as assigned
Support and deliver on key transformation strategies (i.e. Ivalua implementation) of the group, as a priority.

Qualifications:
Excellent analytical skills with the ability to develop complex cost model and come up with actionable cost-optimization recommendations
5+ years of progressive business experience in strategic sourcing and/or global supply chain related to commercial print or digital communication
In-depth knowledge of sourcing and procurement principles and best practices
Experience with sourcing and procurement systems and tool and Technical and professional knowledge – demonstrated understanding and application of procurement and contracting principles and best practices
Willing to work in on night shift

item.description:
locations
Quezon City
time type
Full time
posted on

JR24090518
The incumbent is responsible for managing the Fieldglass Procurement Operations Support Team in MBPS while improving existing processes to drive operational excellence. Focusing on continuous improvement of process, tools, and people to keep develop the evolve the overall capabilities of the team. Reporting directly to the AVP, Head of Procurement Shared Services, the Manager, Fieldglass Support is primarily accountable for leading the teams in ensuring timely and accurate service delivery.
Position Responsibilities:
Leadership / Management Manage teams and coordinate with multiple business units on daily activities and projects related to Sourcing, Contract Drafting, Vendor Management, Source to Pay and Vendor Onboarding, Contract repository, Governance, and controls,
Build out effective and efficient operations by continuously improving operational effectiveness and motivate teams to achieve service excellence
Pro-actively establish and implement service, quality and productivity initiatives for teams continuous improvement Translate operational business strategy into workable plans for team execution while setting a clear direction, processes, resource allocation, and accountabilities
Perform managerial duties such as goal-setting, hiring, attendance/incentives management, billing, and overall coordination with the support departments such as HR, Finance and IT Ensure that all Service Level Agreements and Statement of Work (SOW) between MBPS and Business Unit comply with the standards and are met at all times
Improve team effectiveness by implementing, monitoring and reviewing training policies and guidelines Manage and maintain all contracts, contract policies and procedures and document tracking and reporting requirements
Stakeholder Management and Change Management Lead Manage a strong strategic working relationship with business unit contacts Provide effective and regular communication with all stakeholders
Lead the regular business reviews and produce dashboard and metrics reports detailing the team’s performance (productivity, utilization, and attendance), challenges and areas for improvement.
Collaborate with senior leaders on strategic planning, leadership and identifying opportunities for the business to improve performance
Lead integrated change management efforts (e.g., communications, training) across the end-to-end process of procurement operations
Lead and coordinate with procurement operations leaders in identifying, designing and implementing approved improvement initiatives as well as providing regular coaching on initiatives led by others
Required Qualifications:
Minimum 7 years related to similar role and responsibilities in a Procurement team.
Bachelor’s degree in law, Supply Chain, Business, or Finance MBA degree is preferable
Excellent interpersonal and negotiation skills and the ability to develop close working relationships and work collaboratively with internal and external customers
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills and the ability to develop close working relationships with all levels within the organization
Superior people management and organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail and result-driven
Strong customer focus, can-do attitude and a passion for producing high-quality work
Trustworthy with high standards of personal integrity Adaptable and able to thrive in a fast-paced environment
Ability to manage risk, evaluate priorities and manage a high volume of work Ability to draft/review contracts
Skills: Must be a procurement expert along with working knowledge on contractual law. Experience implementing Procurement Purchase to Pay, Sourcing and Travel and Expense Audit Knowledge of Concur expense tool preferred but not required Professional experience in a shared service operations model Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Hands-on team leadership and management experience Proficient with MS Excel, Word, SharePoint
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

item.title: Manulife: Mobile Software Engineer
item.description:
locations
Singapore
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: December 31, 2024 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100473
At Manulife, we are dedicated to delivering cutting-edge solutions that enhance the customer experience. Our team is passionate about creating seamless and innovative mobile applications that meet the needs of our customers. We are currently seeking a talented and enthusiastic Mobile Software Engineer to join our team and contribute to our customer mobile app development project.
Job Responsibilities:
Design and Development:
Design, develop, and maintain high-performance hybrid mobile applications using Angular and CapacitorJS.
Implement responsive and adaptive user interfaces to ensure seamless user experiences across various devices and screen sizes.
Develop reusable, modular, and maintainable components and services in Angular.
Utilize CapacitorJS plugins to integrate native functionalities such as camera, geolocation, push notifications, and biometric authentication.
Adhere to industry best practices, coding standards, and design patterns to ensure high-quality code.
Conduct thorough code reviews and provide constructive feedback to peers.
Integration and Testing:
Integrate RESTful APIs and third-party services into the mobile applications.
Ensure secure and efficient data exchange between the mobile application and backend services.
Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests to ensure the reliability and robustness of the mobile applications.
Debug and resolve issues, performance bottlenecks, and security vulnerabilities.
Set up and maintain CI/CD pipelines to automate the build, test, and deployment processes for mobile applications.
Collaborate with DevOps teams to ensure smooth deployment and release cycles.
Mobile Security:
Implement security best practices to protect the application and user data from various threats and vulnerabilities.
Ensure compliance with industry security standards and regulations.
Utilize encryption techniques to protect sensitive data both at rest and in transit.
Implement secure authentication and authorization mechanisms, including multi-factor authentication (MFA) and biometric authentication.
Conduct regular security audits and penetration testing to identify and mitigate potential security risks.
Collaborate with security teams to address vulnerabilities and ensure ongoing security improvements.
Collaboration and Documentation:
Work closely with UX/UI designers, product managers, and other stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into technical specifications.
Participate in agile ceremonies such as sprint planning, daily stand-ups, and retrospectives.
Document technical designs, APIs, and implementation details for future reference and knowledge sharing.
Create user guides and technical documentation to assist end-users and support teams.

How will you create impact?
The mobile software engineer will report to the Engineering Lead and will work closely with the mobile development team, product managers, and other cross-functional teams. This role plays a crucial part in shaping the user experience and functionality of our mobile applications. The mobile software engineer will contribute to the ongoing success and growth of our mobile platform, directly impacting our user base and overall business objectives. The work of this role will help drive innovation and ensure that our mobile applications remain at the forefront of technology and user satisfaction.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Proven experience in building hybrid mobile applications using Angular and CapacitorJS.
Strong understanding of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript/TypeScript.
Experience with mobile development frameworks like Ionic, React Native is a plus.
Familiarity with native mobile development (iOS/Android) is advantageous.
Strong understanding of mobile security best practices and experience implementing security features.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and collaboration skills.

What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.
About Manulife and John Hancock

End Date: December 31, 2024 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24040165
The Opportunity

The Multi Asset Solutions Team (MAST) manages over $171 billion of multi-manager, multi-strategy portfolios distributed across the Institutional, Retail, Retirement and High Net Worth platforms at Manulife.

The Global Macroeconomic Strategy Team is hiring a Global Macro Strategist. Reporting directly to the Global head of Macro Strategy, this person will help develop views on the economic and capital markets environment that will support investment recommendations within multi-asset solutions (MAST) and Manulife Investment Management (MIM).

While the role’s area of focus will be on the Emerging Markets (Asia), key responsibilities will include helping develop global economic forecasts and generating risk scenarios that will be used for asset class return forecasting. More broadly, the person will support the Macro Strategy team and Chief Economist in enhancing economic and policy views and pushing the team’s economic research agenda forward

Responsibilities
Contribute to the development of global macroeconomic strategy views that directly impact investment decisions and portfolio performance on a wide array of multi-asset investment products as well as Manulife Investment Management’s multiple business lines.
Engage with global portfolio management teams on investment themes and ideas; demonstrate interest and development in the integration of macroeconomic strategy into actionable market views.
Support senior Macro Strategy and MAST members with macroeconomic and market research, the development of economic forecasts, improvement of models and processes, and managing the group’s extensive chart repository.
Support the firm in client facing, thought leadership, and brand building efforts that would include but are not limited to written pieces, one-on-one and group presentations, and media appearances.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.
What We Are Looking For
Bachelor's degree required; Master’s degree preferred
Minimum of 5-7 years of investment/economics experience
Experience in econometric modelling and forecasting
Experience in emerging markets
Proficiency in Matlab, Python, or other languages an asset
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.
Our commitment to you
Values-first culture We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.

item.title: Manulife: Director, Competitive Intelligence & Insights – GWAM Asia
item.description:
locations
Hong Kong
Singapore
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: December 31, 2024 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24080351
The Opportunity / Job Description
Global Wealth & Asset Management (GWAM) is a key growth segment for Manulife, operating through three different channels (Retail, Retirement and Institutional) across North America, Asia and Europe. The business operates externally under the Manulife Investment Management (MIM) brand. As of December 31, 2023, GWAM had a total of $1,054B in Assets Under Management and Administration.
Our Corporate Purpose is to “Make Decisions Easier and Lives Better” and we are dedicated to helping our individual and institutional customers achieve their financial goals. With a global presence, leading investment capabilities in both public and private markets and a commitment to sustainability, GWAM is in a unique position to offer a wide range of solutions to customers around the world.
Reporting to the AVP Competitive Intelligence & Insights, the Director, Competitive Intelligence & Insights GWAM Asia is a critical partner to help Manulife maintain competitive advantage and drive overall business growth in our Asia GWAM business. This role will partner across the team’s other Competitive Intelligence Directors to provide Asia specific competitive intelligence to form the holistic view of our GWAM market and competitor landscape. As part of the GWAM strategy team, the candidate will serve as a trusted advisor to the GWAM AVP strategy leads within the GWAM strategy team, the executive leadership teams, and other internal partners, providing key Asia market and competitor insights to support the development and execution of our strategy.
Responsibilities:
Gather, analyze, and interpret GWAM Asia market and competitor data (including existing and new markets) to provide actionable insights to support GWAM Asia strategic planning and tactical decision-making.
Proactively seek out and monitor industry trends, competitive activities, and market developments to identify opportunities and threats in the Asia GWAM competitive landscape.
Build and maintain strong and productive relationships with cross-functional teams (i.e. Strategy, Finance, business leaders, etc.) to ensure competitive intelligence is a partner in driving key decisions.
Connect industry and competitor intel to Manulife’s internal capabilities and strategies to provide actionable insights to the Asia business leadership, broader GWAM and MFC Executive Leadership, and Board of Directors.
Identify opportunities for cross-pollination of Asia GWAM intelligence and actions across the GWAM strategy and insights team to amplify the impact of our data and research.
Prepare and present cyclical competitive intelligence reports and findings to key stakeholders and executive leadership.
Make recommendations on adjusting data sources, quality, and structure to evolve our intelligence capabilities.
Work with our shared services offshore function to streamline data collection, reporting, and analysis.
What we are looking for
8+ years of competitive intelligence experience in financial services, with deep knowledge and experience in the Asia GWAM business.
Professional designation (MBA, CPA, CFA) an asset.
Excellent strategic thinking and problem-solving abilities, with the ability to synthesize complex information and provide actionable insights.
Ability to connect the dots and rapidly identify and surface the relevant patterns and issues.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and driving to an answer.
Result-oriented with an ability to multi-task in a fast-paced, dynamic environment, prioritize across multiple initiatives, drive accountability, and develop creative solutions where blueprints may not exist.
Outstanding communication and presentation skills with demonstrated ability to articulate complex business insights in simple and impactful words, across diverse audiences.
Ability to develop a clear position / point of view on key questions and issues impacting the business.
Ability to work independently, get to the relevant information and cut through silos.
Team player, collaborative, self-starter, well-organized, innovative, and high-level of initiative.
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits package.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.
Our commitment to you
Values-first culture - we lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity - we create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation - we invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion - we foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship - we build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer

Are you looking for a supportive and collaborative workplace with great benefits and clear career development? You’ve come to the right place.
Why choose Manulife?
Competitive Salary packages and performance bonuses
Day 1 HMO + FREE coverage for your dependents (inclusive of same-sex partners)

Rewarding culture that values wellness and well-being
Performance Bonus
Global network of industry experts
Extensive training resources
Job Description:
We’re looking for an Underwriting Analyst to join our New Business, Underwriting, and Claims team at MBPS. In this role, you are responsible for applying underwriting guidelines and practices, financial agreements and knowledge of group benefits' practices and policies in order to prepare renewals and make recommendations for establish pricing for group plans.
Have the skills and knowledge for the job? Learn more about the opening below!
Key Responsibilities:
Using Manulife designed Excel applications, gather, and manipulate Premium & Claims information for renewals.
Working knowledge of Mainframe (RDB), Merit, and Manuconnect
Completing the HCSA (Health Care Spending Account) or LSA (Lifestyle Savings Account) on a BAR (Billed in Arrears) basis only Combined Contract & CFA for groups as assigned.
Validate renewal information based on established underwriting guidelines and practices.
Using additional pricing tools needed to complete a renewal using the manual renewal tools
Coordinate appropriate peer review of completed renewals and if required, ensure changes are incorporated in the renewal not sure I follow this completely, this role will action specific non risk items and pass to an underwriter for risk items/completion of renewal.
Produce a variety of reports (i.e. Experience, Drug claims, etc…) using Manulife Reporting tools
Produce high quality work that requires great attention to detail.
Qualifications:
Minimum Experience
College or University graduate of any course
Must have at least one year tenure in current position
Should not have any attendance issues or any disciplinary action
Meet job expectations and pass all performance metrics in his/her current unit
Technical Skills
Ability to use the desktop computer system
Booting and shutting down computer, using mouse and keyboard
Toggling between multiple applications during each transaction
Ability to use email
Composing and sending emails across GB departments and levels
Proficient in the use of Microsoft. Excel, and power point
Soft Skills
Excellent comprehension, analytical, problem solving, and decision-making skills
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Ability to compose a grammatically correct, concise, and accurate paragraph or request
Ability to determine errors
Ability to proactively respond to situations (escalations)
Decision making
Influencing others to act in the best interest of the clients
Ability to multitask and handle different inquiries
Product Specific Knowledge
Good knowledge on Underwriting end-to-end process
Ability to understand simple insurance process or principles
Additional information regarding Group Benefits is an advantage
Able to follow product and process protocol
Able to determine the errors and provide action plans for each
Able to create Plan Design Templates and run Net Manual Rates
Work Set-Up
Amenability to work on a hybrid set up and on a mid-shift schedule.
Let's make every day better together. Learn about our opportunities at JOBS.MANULIFE.COM

item.description:
locations
Hong Kong
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: December 31, 2024 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100099
Voice of Customer Manager plays a crucial role to our Digital, Customer Leader ambition. Leveraging your analytical expertise and problem-solving mindset, you will transform customer feedback into actionable insights and recommendations to continuously elevate our customer experience by removing customer irritants and driving value for the business.
You will utilise data from multiple sources to enable our Asia MIM leaders to make informed decisions focusing on process and experience improvements of our Retirement business. By uncovering the unmet needs of customers, you will inform the design of delightful customer experiences that contribute to drive customer loyalty and improve our Net Promoter Score, one of the OKRs of our organisation.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers and listen, engage and act for their benefit
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your analytical skills to enable business outcomes
You thrive in teams, and enjoy getting things done together
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
We are looking for someone:
5+ years of experiences in Customer Experience (including Customer Journey), Customer Research and Insights, Digital Experience, Data Analysis and Project Management
Degree in Marketing, Business or Statistics, with strong marketing and business background
Understand Asia retirement business, such as MPF in Hong Kong, DPLK in Indonesia
Self-starter with strong desire to advance skillset through new tools & self-learning
Team player with excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Proficient in analytical, problem solving and solutioning skills
Experienced in analysing and interpreting a mix of qualitative and quantitative data
Ability to clearly articulate complex ideas and construct compelling narratives to diverse audiences, from business to technical
Comfortable with ambiguity, ability to adapt to changing business requirements and processes
Demonstrate good judgement during issue identification and escalation processes
Experienced in leading, designing and managing end-to-end customer experience projects, e.g., Net Promoter Score
Proficient in written and spoken English and Cantonese
Nice to Have:
Hands-on experience in designing and conducting interviews with customers stakeholders
Working knowledge of data mining, and advanced statistical methods
Familiar with customer experience & feedback platforms, e.g., Qualtrics, Medallia, Survey Monkey, UserTesting, Discuss.io
Advanced Excel skills, with programming and statistical software experience an asset.
Hands-on experience with Microsoft Power Platform, e.g., Power BI, Power Automate
Experience in using digital analytic tools for data analysis, e.g., as Adobe Analytics
Proficient in written and spoken Mandarin
On the job you will:
Dedicate to support Asia Retirement Business
Monitor and analyse customer feedback into emerging themes to inform process and customer experience improvement opportunities across touch points / value streams.
Synthesise multiple sources of customer feedback, customer data, web analytics and operational metrics to transform data into actionable recommendations that make real impact.
Accelerate NPS System maturity by closely collaborate with touch points / value stream owners on an on-going basis.
Work with data office / IT and vendors to implement processes and controls to ensure customer feedback data integrity and accuracy throughout the organisation.
Lead global reporting and in-market dashboard prototype development to democratise insights that enable the organisation to gain deeper understanding of customer needs & irritants.
Support in the development of analytically rich solutions and implementation of emerging tools that help to diagnose problems and anticipate what could be improved moving forward
Maintain close working relationships with Global / Regional teams and implement best practices to support our customer experience vision
Our commitment to you
Our mission; to be a part of making Decisions Easier and Lives Better
A leadership team dedicated to your growth and success
A bold ambition and set of goals to be a leader in driving transformation in our industry
Our best. Every day.
Learn more about opportunities with us at www.manulife.com/en/careers/why-join-us.html
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognise and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

Business Systems Analyst/L2 Application Support - Data Integration
Manulife Financial Corporation / GWAM Technology
Manulife/John Hancock is an innovative global financial services company with offices throughout North America, Asia and Europe. We have a strong history, welcoming our first customers in 1887, and since then have consistently focused on providing customers with strong, reliable, trustworthy and forward-thinking solutions for their most significant financial decisions. Our GWAM (Global Wealth Asset Management) Technology Team develops innovative solutions to complex business problems that add value to our business and for our customers. Using the latest processes & technology, and helmed by a technology-obsessed leadership team, we create and deliver leading-edge systems, products and services that propel our business forward.
This position is for a Operation Analyst in the L2 Application System Support Team. The successful applicant will be involved with an Agile, high performing team doing application support, application deployment, business requirement gathering and data analysis all in support of solutions that conform to Manulife standards. The candidate will need to have a breadth and depth of experience that will allow her/him to devise and support solutions that integrate appropriately within the organization and its application frameworks.
Work Arrangement: Hybrid Schedule (5 days; 9 hours a day: 3 days’ Work on site) Schedule: Rotating Shift (Day, Mid, Night)
People Leader Role: No
Responsibilities:
Work with upstream and downstream systems to debug or trouble shoot data processing issues
Investigate SQL stored procedures, Informatica Workflows, CA WLA failures and Perl scripts from a support perspective to debug issues or perform analysis for minor enhancements
Tracking and resolving a high number of exceptions on a day to day basis as operations support
Using Agile methodologies to track work and enhancements in JIRA.
Coordinate development efforts with the development team for major enhancements
Coordinate code deployments with various teams and ensure audit standards are met
Help shape the future of technology within our group by moving to cloud based solutions.
Remain current on standards, technologies, approaches and methodologies
Consistently apply the concept of continuous improvement
Qualifications:
Minimum 2 years working experience supporting systems at Level 2 (support)
Experience with relational database structures and methodologies
Proven ability to multitask both independently and within a team
Excellent English communication (both written and oral)
Applicable degree or Designation in Computer Science or equivalent experience
Ability to understand business process requirements and, through the use of technology, conceptualize creative solutions through to implementation
Experience in financial industry
Action oriented, creative and proactive thinker
Willing to work different shifts (North American Business Hours + Asia Business Hours)
Successful candidate must have strong knowledge and experience in the following technologies:
MS SQL
Informatica PowerCenter
CA Work Load Automation (or any batch automation tool)
Cloud based technologies (Azure)
PowerBI
Minimum Requirements to Hire:
· EDUCATION: 2-year completed in a Bachelor's program with relevant work experience of at least 2 years; Bachelor’s Degree in Information technology or equivalent degree.
· EXPERIENCE: At least two years of relevant experience
· SCHEDULE: Amenability to work on shifting schedules, weekends, and Philippine holidays (when needed)
· LOCATION: Amenability to work in U.P. Ayala Land Technohub, Quezon City OR Mactan Newtown, Lapu-Lapu City, Cebu
· HYBRID WORK ARRANGEMENT: Amenability and readiness to work onsite and from home anytime (dependent on business need AND/OR current external environment/situation)
Minimum Skills to Hire:
· Minimum 2 years working experience supporting systems at Level 2 (support)
· Experience with relational database structures and methodologies
· Proven ability to multitask both independently and within a team
· Excellent English communication (both written and oral)
· Applicable degree or Designation in Computer Science or equivalent experience
· Ability to understand business process requirements and, through the use of technology, conceptualize creative solutions through to implementation
· Experience in financial industry
· Action oriented, creative and proactive thinker
· Willing to work on night shift (North American Hours)
· Successful candidate must have strong knowledge and experience in the below technologies:
o Intermediate MS SQL
Nice to Haves:
· Successful candidate must have strong knowledge and experience in the below technologies:
o Informatica PowerCenter
o PowerBI
o CA Work Load Automation (or any batch automation tool)
· Successful candidate should have some knowledge of the below two technologies:
o C#
· Cloud based technologies (Azure) – nice to have, not needed. We can train you.

· You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
· You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
· You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
· You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.

Has a natural ability to breakdown, decompose and describe business needs into Stories supporting an iterative and incremental delivery model
Able to communicate effectively verbally and in written form to interact professionally with diverse stakeholders.

What motivates you?

item.description:
locations
Quezon City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply

job requisition id
JR24090627
Are you looking for a supportive and collaborative workplace with great benefits and clear career development? You’ve come to the right place.
Why choose Manulife?
Competitive Salary packages and performance bonuses
Day 1 HMO + FREE coverage for your dependents (inclusive of same-sex partners)
Retirement savings benefit
Rewarding culture that values wellness and well-being
Performance Bonus
Global network of industry experts
Extensive training resources
Job Description:
We’re looking for an Operations Risk Analyst to join our GRC team at MBPS. In this role, you are expected to set up and develop a data analytics process for control monitoring and reporting.
Have the skills and knowledge for the job? Learn more about the opening below!
Key Responsibilities:
Coordinating and organizing various risk management development and data collection and analytics efforts.
Effectively plan and lead risk-based process mapping sessions with the lines of businesses to create process workflows, identify risk and controls, understand key controls, and identify measurements to validate controls effectiveness.
Develops procedures for identifying, analyzing, summarizing, and reporting operational errors and loss events in accordance with established control breach definitions. Plan to completion and lead the development through implementation and hand-off of tests and associated tools to those who will execute, as assigned.
Develop data analytics capability to identify operational and process gaps.
Qualifications:
Bachelor's degree, preferably in Finance, Accounting, Computer Science, Business Administration/Management
Working Knowledge of Power Platform (Power BI, Power Pivot, Power Automate, and Power App)
At least three (3) to five (5) years of experience in the insurance industry preferred
Amenability and readiness to work onsite and from home anytime (dependent on business need AND/OR current external environment/situation)

The opportunity

To act as the focal point for the Canadian Business Units or Stakeholders for providing response to client questionnaires, audit, and client contract review. Responsibilities
Review client Master Service Agreement and Contracts for IT Security and Risk related clauses.
Support client audits which include managing communications with subject matter experts and preparing documentations, artifacts, and presentations.
Handle and respond to Information Technology (IT) Security and Risk questionnaires, RFPs, inquiries, and due diligence requests from clients.

Enable effective decision making by retrieving and aggregating data from multiple sources and compiling reports for the business unit.
Document processes and workflows and maintain/update the existing process documents.
Responsible for generating, compiling, distributing and maintenance of dashboards and reports.
Work closely with peers to understand and design reports and dashboards to meet analytical requirements.

item.title: Manulife: Director, Asia Operational Risk Management
item.description:
locations
Hong Kong
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 5, 2024 (25 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24090504
The Opportunity
Reporting to AVP, Operational Risk Management (ORM), Asia, this position will support the Asia Risk Management department on various key regional operational risk programs and promoting a risk aware culture and increasing risk awareness through educational initiatives.
Position Responsibilities:
Influence and collaborate with business and functional leaders to ensure effective implementation of various key ORM programs such as dynamic RCSA and embedding operational resilience framework.
Support business units to identify critical business services, setting of impact tolerances, mapping of underlying assets including interconnections and interdependencies, facilitating severe but plausible scenario testing and vulnerability management of the critical business operations
Maintain effective risk governance and management for overseeing operational risk activities within the Segment
Work closely with the country Chief Risk Officers, Compliance and Information Risk to understand existing risk and compliance processes to determine the approach to adapt global and regional program requirements in the Segment
Manage the overall planning and implementation of the ORM program and priorities rollout. Conduct training, facilitate workshops and coordinate reporting for the Segment. Ensure consistent quality of the identified risks and controls, as well as the effective challenge provided by country teams
Offer innovative ideas and help increase the efficiency of business processes and risk management activities
Assist sharing of key learnings, including incidents/ issues learning, risk management/ control best practices across Asia
Required Qualifications:
Risk management or audit background with 10+ years of experience in matters of increasing complexity
Good working knowledge of enterprise and operational risk management frameworks and processes
Hands-on experience in implementing operational resilience programs at other financial institutions
In depth understanding of operational procedures at financial institution (preferably insurance)
Experience in designing, implementing, and validating the effectiveness of operational controls
Highly developed business acumen, and pragmatic approach to solving problems and implementing solutions and a willingness to challenge the status quo
Exceptional communication skills, comfortable in providing presentations and conducting facilitation sessions to different audience groups. Ability to articulate complex issues to a diverse audience
High proficiency in visualization and presentation of concepts and insights to enable key decisions
Strong influencing skills, creative problem solving and ability to drive significant change in a fast-paced, constantly evolving environment
Strong stakeholder and relationship building skills to maintain effective working relationships with business units, various Regional functions, Group office and external parties. Comfortable working with all levels of management
Leadership required to influence and guide local teams to achieve deadlines and to overcome/resolve unforeseen challenges
Maintain a strong sense of compliance, control, and risk awareness
Comfortable with occasional international business travel
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

The Opportunity
Reporting to AVP, Operational Risk Management (ORM), Asia, this position will support the Asia Risk Management on various key regional operational risk programs such as embedding operational resilience framework, ensuring robust governance, and promoting a risk aware culture and increasing risk awareness through educational initiatives.
In this role, the person will lead and manage multiple stakeholders across the functions and Markets in the region and act as subject matter expert to provide guidance and support in implementing the operational resilience program in Asia.
Position Responsibilities:
Influence and closely collaborate with key stakeholders across the functions in local Markets and regional level to lead the implementation and ongoing management of the operational resilience program in Asia
Support business units to identify critical business services, setting of impact tolerances, mapping of underlying assets including interconnections and interdependencies, facilitating severe but plausible scenario testing and vulnerability management of the critical business operations
Support the development and maintenance of the Global Operational Resilience Framework, including policies and procedures, operating model as well as program plans
Ensure operational resilience principles and practices are understood and applied across businesses in Asia. Educate and train business units and regional functions on operational resilience best practices and strategies
Support the development of solutions for strategic changes required for resilience and opportunities for efficiencies across related risk management programs
Develop robust communication channels between local Market level, Regional and Global stakeholders, ensuring transparency, collaboration, and alignment of ORM priorities with business objectives
Ensure robust risk governance and embed appropriate risk and controls across various ORM program activities in Asia
Plan and lead periodic quality assurance reviews to evaluate the effectiveness of governance and risk management activities at local Market level, as well as compliance to Global Policies and Standards. Identify gaps and opportunities to uplift risk maturity
Offer innovative ideas and help increase the efficiency of business processes and risk management activities
Assist sharing of key learnings, including incidents/ issues learning, risk management/ control best practices across Asia
Required Qualifications:
Minimum of 10+ years of experience in financial services industry or/ and management consulting experience required
Hands-on experience in implementing operational resilience programs at other financial institutions
Experience with recovery strategy and requirements, risk assessments, business impact analyses, testing, crisis and incident response management
Knowledge of international operational resilience regulatory requirements for the financial services industry and insurance operations flow is a plus
Highly developed business acumen, and pragmatic approach to solving problems and implementing solutions and a willingness to challenge the status quo
Exceptional communication skills, comfortable in providing presentations and conducting facilitation sessions to different audience groups. Ability to articulate complex issues to a diverse audience

item.title: Manulife: Chief Actuary, Vietnam
item.description:
locations
Dist.7, Ho Chi Minh
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 18, 2024 (7 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24020905
The opportunity
This position is responsible to ensure that all actuarial aspects of financial reporting, and in particular valuation, are consistent with Manulife standards, professional standards and regulatory requirements. The incumbent is expected to play a key leadership role to work closely with Vietnam key stakeholders in driving company strategy and translate financial trends for meaningful discussion and to measurable actions, and also to provide problem solving support on complex business issues. The role has to ensure that adequate management reports and analysis are in place to highlight financial results and identify issues/opportunities for enhancement. The incumbent will also assist in various ad hoc projects including M&A activities, bancassurance deals and new channel strategy. The role will fully engage with Regional Actuarial Services in improving IFRS17 processes to gain efficiencies and an accelerated quarter-end deliverables.

Responsibilities:
Leadership
Create a picture of the future for Manulife, and translate the vision into clear specific, and achievable objectives that engages employees towards a common goal; Create an environment that encourages and motivates individuals to stretch beyond what they thought they could, maintain high standards and role models desired behaviours and actions; Build a high performing team, leverage unique talents of others, and foster trust and credibility and maintain a work-life balance working environment by prioritizing and applying good business judgement to make sound decisions; Adopt various coaching styles and align individuals unique interests, abilities, and motivations with opportunities for growth; and Apply understanding of current and future business needs to attract, develop and retain key talent; shares talent and encourages talent movement across the organization
Financial Management
Provide leadership on various valuations and actuarial investigations required, ensure that appropriate assumptions, consistent methodology, standards and practices are followed. Deliver quality financial report and in particular plays a hand on role with respect to the Source of Earnings analysis, DCAT, Embedded Value, and market risk sensitivity. Provide and communicate local and IFRS 17 sources of earnings analysis and estimates and identify trends and business issues to highlight to Senior Management. Provide and communicate meaningful MIS, analysis and decision support to BU Management Engage with Regional Office to propose improvements to the IFRS 17 processes. Management of the quarter PAR processes and annual basis change process ensuring that adequate experience investigation and review are in place. Execute planning and earnings forecasts process with other key stakeholders in Vietnam Participate in Vietnam ALCO, monitor and manage risk issues relate to valuation processes and earnings impact. Provide timely and accurate reports to external auditor on regulatory reports, and main contact point for the external auditor Perform the statutory duties of Appointed Actuary such as approval of premium rates, Actuary Report, Risk Management Policy and Investment Policy Meet regulatory (MOF) reporting requirements providing accurate results in a timely manner Perform experiences studies and communicate and implement impact of basis change Review reinsurance treaties and ensure execution of treaties Manage the Local capital ratio closely. Communicate monthly movements. Forecast future trends and timing of capital remittances.
Risk Management
Acting as first line of defense, work closely with Chief Risk Officer in identifying, managing and migrating financial risks, capital risk, etc. Provide impact analysis on a timely manner on regulatory solvency and ERM consultations Deliver quality and timely support for valuation control and ensure controls in place and well managed
Pricing
Work closely with Chief Product Officer to oversee and mange product risk Assist Chief Product Officer to ensure the timely and accurately products submissions to MOF
Growth and Business review
Provide financial analysis and advice to business to support growth of distribution channels and other business initiatives. Support the development of consumer/environment/regulatory changes. Regularly provide briefs on impending regulatory changes Play an active role in industry actuarial committees Participate and execute in inorganic strategy such as M&A deals, bancaasurance deals, new channel strategy.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
Fellow of the Canadian Institute of Actuaries, Fellow of Society of Actuaries or Fellow of the Institute of Actuaries.
12+ years of experience with strong business sense
Strong knowledge of Local reserve processes and IFRS17 is a plus
Solid experience in valuation and risk management
Strong analytical and decision making skills
Sound business judgement and strategic thinking ability
Strong leadership and management skills
Demonstrated project management skills
Excellent communication & relationship skills
Strong influential skills
Fluent in Vietnamese is a plus
Good verbal and written English skill

What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

item.description:
locations
Phnom Penh
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 31, 2024 (20 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100574
Job Opportunity
The Activation and Event Manager is responsible for the strategic planning, coordination, and execution of brand activations and events. This role requires a highly organized individual with attention to detail who can manage multiple work areas simultaneously within the marketing team. The goal is to ensure all events effectively promote the brand, engage the identified target audience, and meet business objectives. The ideal candidate will have extensive experience in event management, excellent communication skills, and a keen eye for detail.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.
Position Responsibilities:
Event Planning and Execution:
Develop and execute comprehensive event plans, including timelines, budgets, and logistics with define KPIs/Success indicators - supported by Head of Business/Chief Marketing Officer
Coordinate all aspects of events, including venue selection, vendor management, catering, transportation, entertainment, and staffing in line with event objectives.
Work closely with each workstream lead withing marketing team to develop and implement promotional strategies and materials for events effectively and on time.
Lead team meeting to provide regular updates on event progress to keep working team on the right track and to remove roadblocks leading up to event date.
Brand and Product Activations:
Design and implement brand activation strategies to increase brand awareness, consideration and engagement OR as direction defined by HOB.
Collaborate with marketing and creative teams to develop activation concepts that align with the brand’s identity and objectives.
Assist head of brand to develop compelling marketing campaign to launch and promote the product and propositions.
Oversee the production and installation of activation/branding materials and setups.
Vendor Management:
Serve as the main point of contact for vendors or other event partners, ensuring clear and consistent communication and successful execution of all events and activations.
Responsible for vendor selections according to expertise, background, experience and budget
Responsible for vendor registration, payment and other processes according to company policies
Budget & Report Management:
Develop and manage event budgets, ensuring all expenses are tracked and reported accurately.
Negotiate contracts with vendors and suppliers to achieve the best possible terms and rates (with support from procurement)
Monitor event expenditures and identify cost-saving opportunities.
Conduct post-event evaluations to assess the success of events and identify areas for improvement.
Prepare detailed reports summarizing event outcomes, including attendance, engagement, and ROI.
Ensure all events and activations are delivered on time, within scope, and within budget.
Required qualifications:
A minimum of 3 years of experience in event & activation, marketing & communications, public relation and/or in related field.
Experience in developing event and activation plan for various event and occasions including internal and external.
Good at strategic planning, coordination, and execution of brand activations and events to effectively convey the organization's brand, product and service.
Excellent at project management skill, with the ability to work with various stakeholders to ensure all events effectively promote the brand, engage the identified target audience, and meet business objectives
Extensive experience in event management, excellent communication skills, and a keen eye for details.
Highly organized individual with attention to detail who can manage multiple work areas simultaneously within the marketing team.
Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams, external partners, and community stakeholders.
A proactive and adaptable mindset, with the ability to thrive in a dynamic and fast-paced environment while maintaining a focus on achieving strategic objectives.
Creativities & proactiveness and Multi-tasking.

item.title: Manulife: Actuarial Analyst
item.description:
locations
Quezon City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: December 31, 2024 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24051774
Are you looking for a supportive and collaborative workplace with great benefits and clear career development? You’ve come to the right place.
Why Join MBPS Actuarial?
Since its establishment in 2012, the MBPS Actuarial team has grown into Manulife's Actuarial Center of Excellence and has become the country's largest Actuarial employer. Their unwavering dedication to precision and ability to distill complex data into actionable insights has allowed them to continuously set the bar higher with their industry-defining expertise in Valuation, Liability and Asset Modelling, Experience Analytics, Pricing, and more.
MBPS Actuarial offers a wide range of global opportunities across multiple actuarial disciplines, a high performing culture, a market leading actuarial development program, a job rotation program, and a leadership team dedicated to your growth and success. Beyond that, their influence extends to the broader community and industry, enriching the professional landscape for everyone involved.
Job Description:
We’re looking for a talented and driven Actuarial Analyst to join our Actuarial Function at MBPS. In this role, you will work closely with our Actuarial team to collect, analyze, and interpret data that drive key business decisions. With our team’s long-established history of success, you will be embark on a rewarding and engaging career path that exposes you to different facets of our global organization.
Have the skills and knowledge for the job? Learn more about the opening below!
NOTE: The final job title and level you receive will be dependent on your years of experience and your interviews during the application process.
Key Responsibilities:
Collaborate with our skilled team of actuaries to support the company’s strategic data analysis, risk assessment, and business planning
Support the business in tasks that may include the following:
Pricing
Portfolio Modeling
Asset Modeling
Valuation
Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure precise financial projections, optimal pricing strategies, and effective risk-management solutions
Drive innovation by staying at the forefront of industry trends, regulatory shifts, and technological advancements
Qualifications:
Bachelor’s Degree in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Finance or related field
At least 1 year of actuarial work experience in Insurance or Financial Services. Fresh graduates are welcome to apply and will be considered for the Actuarial Assistant role.
Progress towards attaining a designation in any recognized actuarial organization (SOA, ASP)
Working experience in MS Office (Excel, VBA, Access), GGY AXIS, SQL, Qlikview, Python or similar program is a plus
Proven analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to gather, synthesize, organize, and report information, results, and recommendations to various levels across the organization
Strong ability to establish priorities and manage multiple tasks with accuracy and attention to detail
Able to work in an evolving and dynamic environment
Strong communication skills with the ability to build camaraderie across levels and business functions
Passion to grow and driven to be recognized as a trusted business partner.
Ready to join our high-performing team of Actuarial experts? Apply today!
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Working Arrangement
Hybrid

item.title: Manulife: Customer Acquisition Manager
item.description:
locations
Makati City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 1, 2024 (21 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100174
Job Posting Description
The opportunity
With acquisition being one of the important pillars under Growth Marketing, the Customer Acquisition Manager is a new position that will be responsible for overseeing the entire lead lifecycle, from generation through conversion. This role will augment the 2 positions handling the other 2 pillars, namely, Customer Portfolio Management & Engagement and MarTech.
Responsibilities:
Owns the lead or prospective customer acquisition strategy and end-to-end lead management process, which covers campaign conceptualization/ designing, implementation, monitoring & post-evaluation
Leverages on performance marketing and data analytics insights to identify new lead generation opportunities and/or optimize the marketing funnel
Formulate and design campaigns to capture, nurture and convert leads / prospects
Lead the cross-functional teams (e.g. CX and propositions) to develop the ideal lead/ prospect journey to better connect, nurture and engage them and drive greater marketing and sales effectiveness
Partners with performance marketing and analytics team to manage and optimize the entire leads lifecycle: from generation of campaign leads, A/B testing, tracking, maintenance and enhancement of the leads dashboard to evaluate campaign effectiveness/efficiency
Co-develop and execute holistic change management programs with the distribution channels (e.g. agency and bank partner) to drive leads utilization and conversion
While applying a customer-centric, insights-driven approach, develops and sets the funnel requirements with the technology team: leads allocation / distribution through Manulife platforms, management of user acceptance and usability testing, real-time leads funnel tracking and reporting
Individual Accountabilities:
Accountable for overseeing the entire lead lifecycle, from generation through conversion
Accountable for delivering the KPIs/ metrics (e.g. lead conversion, lead count, etc.)
Key Shared Accountabilities:
Together with Performance Marketing and Analytics, accountable for generating campaigns, identifying lead gen opportunities, enabling end to end lead performance tracking
Together with IS and Distribution, accountable for enabling a seamless lead allocation/ distribution via Manulife platforms
Together with Distribution, accountable for crafting and execution of change management initiatives to ensure program adoption by our distributors
How will you create impact?

The position will report to Growth Marketing Lead and will focus on contributing to APE production by driving leads acquisition, nurturing and conversion.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
College degree (bachelor’s degree in any major)
At least 3 to 5 years of relevant experience in leads acquisition / management, customer retention, engagement or lifecycle management, marketing & business development or other related functions
Demonstrated strength in strategic and analytical thinking, strong financial and business acumen is a plus
Showcased strength in project management through previous formal or informal positions of working with cross-functional teams
Demonstrated ability to effectively lead change
Flexible, results oriented and able to work in a fast-paced, ever-changing environment
Strong interpersonal and communication skills (oral and written)
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision
A self-starter with an ability to multi-task
Knowledgeable in MS Office Applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
